fields.description:
# Comprehensive live reconciler validation + guaranteed bilateral cleanup

## Why
This epic's correctness is argued from code + an OSS/commercial comparison (session log spirited-balsamic-sealion)
but never PROVEN end-to-end against live Jira. An agent with ACLI access validates the full bidirectional surface
on live Jira and GUARANTEES cleanup of every test artifact in BOTH systems.

## Signal definitions (what each assertion observes)
- C1 (conflict signal): differ emits a conflict record (`reason="conflict"`) when local!=baseline AND jira!=baseline on a mirrored field (feline-wandering-dassierat).
- C2 (hard-delete -> re-create): after a Jira hard-delete, a NEW issue is created with the SAME `rebar-id:<local_id>` label + entity property and bound; NO false "re-created" comment (lowerclass-creased-fruitbat).
- C3 (field-allowlist-drop alert): a deduped BRIDGE_ALERT fires when an outbound field is dropped by the update allowlist (nonglacial-tense-vulture).
- C4 (429/Retry-After): a synthetic 429 (monkey-patched adapter returns 429 + `Retry-After: 2` on the first call) -> reconciler backs off >=2s and retries successfully (lambswool-shaded-eider).
- Echo suppression: outbound comments carry `<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->` AND field/status writes are baseline-seeded via ProvenanceLedger (NOT a literal mark) — verify by `mutation_count==0` on the next pass over the just-written data.

## Harness (guaranteed bilateral teardown)
- pytest autouse fixture with `yield` + `finally` (or bash `trap cleanup EXIT`) that hard-deletes Jira test issues
  AND discards local test tickets — runs even on assertion failure/exception.
- Teardown-failure fallback: each cleanup call is retry-with-backoff (3x); on exhaustion, append the artifact id to
  `leaked-artifacts.log` and exit non-zero so a leak is LOUD, never silent.
- Setup probe: create one throwaway issue and assert `client.delete_issue` returns `{"status":"deleted"}` (NOT a 403
  soft-fail — `acli.py:716-720` swallows PermissionError) so teardown can actually delete.

## Isolation, credentials, blast-radius
- Dedicated Jira TEST project; a dedicated service account whose "Delete Issues" permission is scoped to that
  project only (project role, not global). Token injected via a secret/secrets manager; never logged, never committed.
- Local side: an ISOLATED throwaway tickets env/branch (worktree) so binding-store commits + ticket events are
  discarded post-run (append-only events must not pollute production history). Do NOT rely on local delete.
- Blast-radius breaker test: provision >=11 synthetic bindings so one acting decision = fraction 0.09 (no trip on
  normal) and a synthetic mass-change trips it; OR override the max-acting-fraction for the test.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Harness with GUARANTEED bilateral teardown (finally/fixture); a teardown-under-failure test injects a RuntimeError after create and asserts BOTH artifacts are still cleaned.
- [ ] One observable pass criterion PER matrix scenario (C1-C4, echo, status round-trip incl. lossy labels, tombstone/grace, idempotency mutation_count==0, blast-radius trip); harness exits 0 all-green and attaches a JSON/JUnit report to the ticket.
- [ ] Teardown-failure fallback: retry-with-backoff, `leaked-artifacts.log` + non-zero exit on any leak; ZERO surviving artifacts on success (Jira hard-deleted; local throwaway env discarded).
- [ ] Delete-permission probe passes (delete_issue returns deleted, not 403 soft-fail); credential scoped to the test project + injected via secret.
- [ ] Isolation documented (test project + throwaway local env); production REB + tickets branch untouched.
- [ ] Re-runnable: a second run reproduces the same green report + clean sweep (idempotent teardown).
